title: dactylorhiza majalis subsp. brennensis
intro: Dactylorhiza majalis subsp. brennensis, also known as the Austrian Marsh Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza majalis subsp. brennensis include potential applications in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its use in traditional remedies for skin and wound healing. Dactylorhiza majalis subsp. brennensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tinctures, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine and homeopathic practices.
